description:
Implement pre-key and one-time-key storage in src/keys/.

Functions to implement:
- savePreKey(db, userID: string, deviceID: string, publicKey: string, signature: string, index: number): Promise<void>
- getPreKeys(db, userID: string): Promise<PreKey[]>
- saveOTK(db, userID: string, deviceID: string, publicKey: string, signature: string, index: number): Promise<void>
- getOTK(db, deviceID: string): Promise<OneTimeKey | null> — atomically fetches and deletes one OTK
- getOTKCount(db, deviceID: string): Promise<number>
- getKeyBundle(db, deviceID: string): Promise<KeyBundle | null>
  - Returns { device: Device, preKey: PreKey, otk: OneTimeKey | null }
  - OTK is consumed (deleted) as part of retrieval
  - Returns null if device not found

Zod schemas (src/keys/schemas.ts):
- PreKeySchema, OneTimeKeySchema, KeyBundleSchema
- PublicKeySchema: z.string().regex(/^[0-9a-f]{64}$/)
- SaveOTKBodySchema: array of { publicKey, signature, index }
title: Implement cryptographic key management

description:
Write vitest tests for the WebSocket connection manager before implementation.

Tests (use ws in test mode or mock the ws.WebSocket class):
- Challenge is sent to client immediately on connection (32 random bytes)
- Valid signature (tweetnacl sign.detached.verify) authenticates the client
- Invalid signature disconnects the client
- Message over 2048 bytes is rejected and client is disconnected
- Ping is sent every 5 seconds; client not responding to pong is disconnected
- Authenticated client is registered by deviceID
- Message with 'mail' resource type is dispatched correctly
- Disconnect removes client from registry
- Sending to an offline device (no registered client) does not throw
title: [TEST] WebSocket connection manager tests

description:
Write vitest tests for the three utility modules before implementation.

src/utils/logger.ts tests:
- createLogger('myservice') returns a pino logger instance
- Logger has the service name bound as metadata

src/utils/uuid.ts tests:
- createUUID() returns a valid UUID v4 string
- uuidToUint8('xxxxxxxx-xxxx-...') returns a Uint8Array of length 16
- uuidToUint8 is the inverse of UUID → string conversion

src/config.ts tests:
- Parsing valid env vars returns correctly typed config object
- DB_TYPE='sqlite' with no DATABASE_URL is valid
- DB_TYPE='postgres' requires DATABASE_URL or throws ZodError
- Missing SPK throws ZodError with clear message
- API_PORT coerces string '3000' to number 3000
- LOG_LEVEL defaults to 'info' when not set
- NODE_ENV defaults to 'development' when not set
title: [TEST] Utility function tests

description:
Write vitest tests for the invite system before implementation.

Test cases (in-memory SQLite via Kysely):
- createInvite(db, serverID, ownerID, expiration) inserts invite row
- getInvite(db, inviteID) returns invite or null
- getServerInvites(db, serverID) returns all invites for a server
- deleteInvite(db, inviteID) removes the invite
- Expired invite: isInviteValid(invite) returns false if expiration < now
- Valid invite: isInviteValid(invite) returns true if expiration > now or expiration is null
title: [TEST] Invite system tests

description:
Write vitest tests for device management before implementation.

Test cases (in-memory SQLite via Kysely):
- registerDevice(userID, signKey, name) creates device row
- registerDevice rejects duplicate signKey (unique constraint)
- registerDevice rejects invalid signKey (not 64 hex chars)
- getDevice(deviceID) returns device or null
- getUserDevices(userID) returns array of non-deleted devices
- deleteDevice(userID, deviceID) sets deleted=true, not a hard delete
- markDeviceLogin(deviceID) updates lastLogin timestamp
- getDevice on a deleted device returns null (or device with deleted=true depending on contract)
title: [TEST] Device management tests

description:
Implement file, avatar, and emoji upload/serve logic in src/files/.

DB functions (src/files/db.ts):
- createFile(db, ownerID: string, nonce: string): Promise<FileRecord>
- getFile(db, fileID: string): Promise<FileRecord | null>
- createEmoji(db, ownerID: string, name: string): Promise<Emoji>
- getEmoji(db, emojiID: string): Promise<Emoji | null>
- getEmojiList(db, ownerID: string): Promise<Emoji[]>
- deleteEmoji(db, emojiID: string): Promise<void>

Path helpers (src/files/paths.ts):
- getFilePath(fileID: string): string
- getAvatarPath(userID: string): string
- getEmojiPath(emojiID: string): string
- ensureStorageDirs(): Promise<void> — creates files/, avatars/, emoji/ if missing

Validation (src/files/validate.ts):
- ALLOWED_IMAGE_TYPES: Set of allowed MIME types (jpeg, png, gif, apng, avif)
- validateImageMime(mime: string): void — throws if not allowed
- assertUnderLimit(bytes: number, type: 'emoji' | 'file' | 'avatar'): void

Upload handling: use multer for multipart, also support base64 JSON upload path (POST /file/json, POST /avatar/:id/json, POST /emoji/:serverID/json).
Use file-type (ESM, latest) to detect MIME from buffer rather than trusting Content-Type.
title: Implement file, avatar & emoji storage

description:
Create the application entry points for apps/spire.

src/run.ts (entry point)
- FIRST LINE (before any other imports): import { extendZodWithOpenApi } from '@asteasolutions/zod-to-openapi'; extendZodWithOpenApi(z);
  This must happen before any domain schema file is imported, or .openapi() metadata will be missing from the generated spec.
- Import config from ./config.js (parsed + validated via Zod)
- Import and create Kysely DB instance
- Import and start the Express app (HTTP routes + Scalar docs)
- Import and start the WebSocket server
- Handle graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT: close DB connection, close WebSocket server

src/index.ts (barrel exports)
- Export Spire server class (or factory function)
- Export Database type and Kysely instance factory
- Export all public TypeScript types (User, Device, Mail, Server, Channel, Permission, etc.)
- Export generateOpenAPIDocument() — allows consumers to get the spec programmatically

package.json additions:
- 'start': 'node dist/run.js'
- 'build': 'pnpm generate:openapi && tsc -p tsconfig.build.json'
- 'dev': 'node --watch --import tsx/esm src/run.ts'
- Verify 'generate:openapi' and 'lint:openapi' scripts are present (added in vex-chat-l3q)
title: Set up run.ts entry point and index.ts barrel exports

description:
Wire up OpenAPI spec linting as a pre-commit gate using Spectral and Husky.

.spectral.yaml (in apps/spire/):
- extends: ['spectral:oas']
- Disable: info-contact (off)
- Downgrade to warn: operation-description, oas3-parameter-description, tag-description
- Custom rule: assert no x-internal endpoints appear in public spec paths

Husky setup (workspace root):
- Install husky + lint-staged at workspace root (devDependencies)
- npx husky init → creates .husky/pre-commit
- .husky/pre-commit runs: pnpm --filter @vex-chat/spire lint-staged

lint-staged config (apps/spire/.lintstagedrc.json):
{
  'openapi.json': ['spectral lint --fail-severity=error --ruleset .spectral.yaml']
}

This means Spectral only runs when openapi.json is staged — no overhead on unrelated commits.

package.json verification:
- 'lint:openapi': 'spectral lint openapi.json --ruleset .spectral.yaml' is present (added in vex-chat-l3q)

CI drift check (.github/workflows/check-openapi.yml):
- On push/PR when src/** or openapi.json changes
- Step 1: pnpm generate:openapi
- Step 2: git diff --exit-code openapi.json — fails if committed spec doesn't match freshly generated one
- Step 3: spectral lint openapi.json --fail-severity=error
title: Set up Spectral + Husky pre-commit linting for openapi.json

description:
Implement encrypted mail storage and retrieval in src/mail/.

Functions to implement:
- saveMail(db, mail: NewMail): Promise<Mail>
- retrieveMail(db, deviceID: string): Promise<Mail[]>
- deleteMail(db, nonce: string): Promise<void>

Mail is end-to-end encrypted by the client — the server stores the ciphertext opaquely. The server never decrypts mail.

Zod schemas (src/mail/schemas.ts):
- MailSchema: { nonce, recipient, mailID, sender, header, cipher, group, extra, mailType, time, forward, authorID, readerID }
- SendMailBodySchema: z.object({ nonce, recipient, mailID, sender, header, cipher, group?, extra?, mailType, forward?, authorID, readerID })
title: Implement mail system

description:
Implement the WebSocket connection manager (src/ws/ClientManager.ts) using ws directly (no express-ws).

Responsibilities:
- Attach ws.Server to the existing Express http.Server
- Challenge-response authentication flow:
  1. Client connects → server sends random 32-byte challenge
  2. Client signs challenge with their device signing key (NaCl/tweetnacl)
  3. Server verifies signature against stored device signKey
  4. On success, client is registered as authenticated
- Message format: 32-byte header + variable MessagePack body (@msgpack/msgpack). Max total size: 2048 bytes. Hard-reject larger messages.
- Ping/pong keep-alive: 5-second interval, disconnect unresponsive clients
- Device-specific routing: each authenticated connection keyed by deviceID
- Resource dispatch: parse message header to route to correct handler (initially: 'mail' resource type)
- Emit events (EventEmitter) when messages arrive so Spire class can forward to recipients

Power level constants (src/ws/powerLevels.ts):
  INVITE = 25, CREATE = 50, DELETE = 50, EMOJI = 25

Uses: tweetnacl for signature verification, @msgpack/msgpack for encoding/decoding
title: Implement WebSocket connection manager

description:
Implement device management logic in src/devices/.

Functions to implement:
- registerDevice(db, userID: string, signKey: string, name: string): Promise<Device>
  - Validates signKey: must be 64 hex characters
  - Validates userID: must be valid UUID
  - Inserts device row
- getDevice(db, deviceID: string): Promise<Device | null>
- getUserDevices(db, userID: string): Promise<Device[]> — returns only non-deleted devices
- deleteDevice(db, userID: string, deviceID: string): Promise<void> — soft delete (sets deleted=true)
- markDeviceLogin(db, deviceID: string): Promise<void>

Zod schemas (src/devices/schemas.ts):
- DeviceSchema: { deviceID: z.string().uuid(), signKey: z.string().regex(/^[0-9a-f]{64}$/), owner: z.string().uuid(), name: z.string(), lastLogin: z.date().nullable(), deleted: z.boolean() }
- RegisterDeviceBodySchema: { signKey: z.string().regex(/^[0-9a-f]{64}$/), name: z.string() }
title: Implement device management

description:
Write vitest tests defining expected authentication behavior before implementing auth.

Test cases (use in-memory SQLite via Kysely for DB):
- registerUser(username, password) creates user with hashed password (PBKDF2-SHA512)
- registerUser rejects duplicate usernames
- registerUser rejects usernames not matching /^(\w{3,19})$/ (too short, too long, special chars)
- loginUser(username, password) returns a signed JWT on correct credentials
- loginUser returns null/throws on wrong password
- loginUser returns null/throws for unknown username
- Decoded JWT contains userID and username
- JWT expires after 7 days
- generateToken(userID, tokenType) returns a scoped token expiring in 10 minutes
- generateToken accepts types: file, avatar, register, device, invite, emoji, connect
- validateToken(token, tokenType) returns true for valid unexpired token of correct type
- validateToken returns false for wrong type or expired token

Password hashing contract:
- hashPassword(password) → { hash, salt } using PBKDF2 SHA-512, 1000 iterations
- verifyPassword(password, hash, salt) → boolean
title: [TEST] User authentication tests

description:
Implement the permission system in src/permissions/.

Power levels (src/permissions/powerLevels.ts):
  INVITE = 25, CREATE = 50, DELETE = 50, EMOJI = 25

Functions:
- createPermission(db, userID: string, resourceType: string, resourceID: string, powerLevel: number): Promise<Permission>
- getPermissions(db, userID: string): Promise<Permission[]>
- getPermissionsByResource(db, resourceID: string): Promise<Permission[]>
- deletePermission(db, permissionID: string): Promise<void>
- hasPermission(db, userID: string, resourceID: string, minPowerLevel: number): Promise<boolean>
- getGroupMembers(db, channelID: string): Promise<User[]> — finds users with permissions on the channel's parent server

Zod schemas (src/permissions/schemas.ts):
- PermissionSchema: { permissionID, userID, resourceType, resourceID, powerLevel }
- ResourceTypeSchema: z.enum(['server', 'channel'])
title: Implement permission/RBAC

description:
Define the full database schema using Kysely's built-in migration system. Must support both SQLite (better-sqlite3 dialect) and PostgreSQL (pg dialect) via DB_TYPE env var.

Tables to define (11 total):
1. users — userID (uuid PK), username (unique), passwordHash, passwordSalt, lastSeen
2. devices — deviceID (uuid PK), signKey (unique), owner (FK → users), name, lastLogin, deleted (bool)
3. preKeys — keyID (uuid PK), userID (idx), deviceID (unique+idx), publicKey, signature, index
4. oneTimeKeys — keyID (uuid PK), userID (idx), deviceID (idx), publicKey, signature, index
5. mail — nonce (uuid PK), recipient (idx), mailID, sender, header, cipher (text), group, extra, mailType, time, forward, authorID, readerID
6. servers — serverID (uuid PK), name, icon
7. channels — channelID (uuid PK), serverID, name
8. permissions — permissionID (uuid PK), userID (idx), resourceType, resourceID (idx), powerLevel (int)
9. files — fileID (uuid PK), owner (idx), nonce
10. emojis — emojiID (uuid PK), owner (idx), name
11. invites — inviteID (uuid PK), serverID (idx), owner, expiration

Kysely setup:
- Define Database interface (src/db/types.ts) mapping table names to row types — this is the source of truth for all table types
- Create Kysely instance factory (src/db/index.ts) that reads DB_TYPE and returns a typed Kysely<Database>
- SQLite: use better-sqlite3 dialect (kysely + better-sqlite3)
- Postgres: use built-in Kysely pg dialect
- Migrations in src/db/migrations/ using Kysely Migrator

Note: Zod schemas for each domain's row types belong in the domain files, not here. This task is purely schema/migration.
title: Implement DB schema (Kysely migrations — 11 tables, SQLite + Postgres)

description:
Write vitest tests for file/avatar/emoji metadata storage before implementation.

Note: these tests cover the DB metadata layer and path helpers only — not actual file I/O or multer uploads.

Test cases:
- createFile(db, ownerID, nonce) inserts file metadata row
- getFile(db, fileID) returns file metadata or null
- createEmoji(db, ownerID, name) inserts emoji row
- getEmoji(db, emojiID) returns emoji or null
- getEmojiList(db, ownerID) returns all emoji for owner
- deleteEmoji(db, emojiID) removes emoji row
- File path helpers:
  - getFilePath(fileID) returns path like './files/{fileID}'
  - getAvatarPath(userID) returns path like './avatars/{userID}'
  - getEmojiPath(emojiID) returns path like './emoji/{emojiID}'
- MIME type validation: validateImageMime(mime) accepts jpeg/png/gif/apng/avif, rejects others
- File size validation: assertUnderLimit(bytes, type) throws if over limit (emoji: 256KB, file/avatar: 20MB)
title: [TEST] File, avatar & emoji storage tests

description:
Write vitest tests that assert the generated OpenAPI document has the correct shape before the HTTP routes are considered complete.

Tests call generateOpenAPIDocument() with all route files imported, then assert:

Document structure:
- openapi version is '3.1.0'
- info.title and info.version are present
- servers array is non-empty
- components.securitySchemes.bearerAuth is defined

Path coverage — assert these paths exist in the generated spec:
- POST /auth, POST /register, POST /whoami, POST /goodbye
- GET|POST /token/{tokenType}
- GET /user/{id}, GET /user/{id}/devices, GET /user/{id}/permissions, GET /user/{id}/servers
- DELETE /user/{userID}/devices/{deviceID}
- POST /user/{id}/devices, GET /device/{id}
- POST /device/{id}/keyBundle, POST /device/{id}/mail, POST /device/{id}/connect
- GET /device/{id}/otk/count, POST /device/{id}/otk
- GET /server/{id}, POST /server/{name}, DELETE /server/{id}
- POST /server/{id}/channels, GET /server/{id}/channels, DELETE /channel/{id}
- GET /server/{serverID}/permissions, DELETE /permission/{id}
- GET /server/{serverID}/invites, POST /server/{serverID}/invites
- GET /file/{id}, POST /file/, POST /file/json
- GET /avatar/{userID}, POST /avatar/{userID}
- GET /emoji/{emojiID}, POST /emoji/{serverID}
- POST /userList/{channelID}

Schema completeness:
- All paths have at least one response defined
- All paths have an operationId
- Routes requiring auth have security: [{ bearerAuth: [] }]
- Request body schemas are present on POST routes

Spectral integration test (optional):
- Run spectral lint programmatically against the generated doc
- Assert zero errors (warnings allowed)
title: [TEST] OpenAPI spec shape tests

description:
Implement server and channel CRUD in src/servers/.

Functions:
- createServer(db, name: string, icon: string): Promise<Server>
- getServer(db, serverID: string): Promise<Server | null>
- getServers(db): Promise<Server[]>
- deleteServer(db, serverID: string): Promise<void>
- createChannel(db, serverID: string, name: string): Promise<Channel>
- getChannel(db, channelID: string): Promise<Channel | null>
- getChannels(db, serverID: string): Promise<Channel[]>
- deleteChannel(db, channelID: string): Promise<void>
- getUserServers(db, userID: string): Promise<Server[]> — joins permissions table

Zod schemas (src/servers/schemas.ts):
- ServerSchema, ChannelSchema
- CreateServerBodySchema: z.object({ name: z.string().min(1), icon: z.string() })
- CreateChannelBodySchema: z.object({ name: z.string().min(1) })
title: Implement server & channel management

description:
Implement the invite system in src/invites/.

Functions:
- createInvite(db, serverID: string, ownerID: string, expiration: Date | null): Promise<Invite>
- getInvite(db, inviteID: string): Promise<Invite | null>
- getServerInvites(db, serverID: string): Promise<Invite[]>
- deleteInvite(db, inviteID: string): Promise<void>
- isInviteValid(invite: Invite): boolean — checks expiration

Zod schemas (src/invites/schemas.ts):
- InviteSchema: { inviteID, serverID, owner, expiration: z.date().nullable() }
- CreateInviteBodySchema: z.object({ expiration: z.string().datetime().optional() })
title: Implement invite system

description:
Implement all Express HTTP route handlers for apps/spire using express@5. Every route file must also call registry.registerPath() to register its routes into the OpenAPI registry (from src/openapi.ts).

Middleware:
- checkAuth: validates JWT from httpOnly cookie or Authorization header. Uses Zod to parse JWT payload. Attaches typed user to req.
- checkDevice: validates device action token from request. Attaches typed device to req.
- Body parsing: JSON + MessagePack (@msgpack/msgpack) up to 20MB
- Security: helmet, cors (with credentials), cookie-parser
- Logging: pino-http
- Scalar docs: mount @scalar/express-api-reference at GET /docs (theme: 'purple')
- Raw spec: mount GET /openapi.json returning generateOpenAPIDocument()

Route groups (each in its own file under src/routes/):
- auth.ts: POST /auth, POST /register, POST /whoami, POST /goodbye, GET|POST /token/:tokenType
- users.ts: GET /user/:id, GET /user/:id/devices, GET /user/:id/permissions, GET /user/:id/servers, DELETE /user/:userID/devices/:deviceID
- devices.ts: POST /user/:id/devices, GET /device/:id, POST /device/:id/keyBundle, POST /device/:id/mail, POST /device/:id/connect, GET /device/:id/otk/count, POST /device/:id/otk
- servers.ts: GET /server/:id, POST /server/:name, DELETE /server/:id, POST /server/:id/channels, GET /server/:id/channels, DELETE /channel/:id, GET /server/:serverID/permissions, DELETE /permission/:id, GET /server/:serverID/invites, POST /server/:serverID/invites
- files.ts: GET /file/:id, GET /file/:id/details, POST /file/, POST /file/json
- avatars.ts: GET /avatar/:userID, POST /avatar/:userID, POST /avatar/:userID/json
- emoji.ts: GET /emoji/:emojiID, GET /emoji/:emojiID/details, POST /emoji/:serverID, POST /emoji/:serverID/json
- groups.ts: POST /userList/:channelID

Each route file pattern:
1. Define Zod request/response schemas using .openapi({ description, example }) annotations
2. Call registry.registerPath() for each route with request body schema, params, and response schemas
3. Implement the actual Express handler using the same Zod schemas for runtime validation

Request validation: use Zod schemas to validate all params, query, and body before touching the DB.

File limits: emoji 256KB, files/avatars 20MB. Allowed image types: image/jpeg, image/png, image/gif, image/apng, image/avif.

Token types: file, avatar, register, device, invite, emoji, connect (10-minute expiry each)

Optional (dev only): mount express-openapi-validator middleware with validateRequests: true, validateResponses: true to catch spec drift early.
title: Implement HTTP API routes and middleware

description:
Write vitest tests for server and channel CRUD before implementation.

Test cases (in-memory SQLite via Kysely):
- createServer(name, icon) creates a server and returns it
- getServer(serverID) returns server or null for unknown ID
- getServers() returns all servers
- deleteServer(serverID) removes the server
- createChannel(serverID, name) creates a channel linked to server
- getChannel(channelID) returns channel or null
- getChannels(serverID) returns all channels for that server
- deleteChannel(channelID) removes the channel
- getUserServers(userID) returns all servers the user has a permission entry for
title: [TEST] Server & channel management tests

description:
Write vitest tests for encrypted mail before implementation.

Test cases (in-memory SQLite via Kysely):
- saveMail(data) inserts a mail row with all required fields
- retrieveMail(deviceID) returns all mail for that device
- retrieveMail returns empty array when no mail
- deleteMail(nonce) removes the mail row
- Mail nonce must be unique (duplicate nonce is rejected)
- retrieveMail only returns mail for the specified recipient device, not others
title: [TEST] Mail system tests

description:
Implement three foundational utilities for apps/spire/src/utils/ AND the OpenAPI registry.

src/utils/logger.ts
- createLogger(service: string) factory using pino@9
- Pretty output in development (pino-pretty), JSON in production
- Binds service name as default metadata field

src/utils/uuid.ts
- createUUID(): string — wraps crypto.randomUUID() (Node 20+ native)
- uuidToUint8(uuid: string): Uint8Array — needed for WebSocket challenge/response

src/config.ts (top-level config module)
- Zod schema for all environment variables:
  - DB_TYPE: z.enum(['sqlite', 'postgres'])
  - DATABASE_URL: z.string() (postgres connection string, required if DB_TYPE=postgres)
  - SQLITE_PATH: z.string().optional() (defaults to './spire.db' if DB_TYPE=sqlite)
  - SPK: z.string() (server signing private key as hex, used as JWT secret)
  - API_PORT: z.coerce.number().default(16777)
  - LOG_LEVEL: z.enum(['trace','debug','info','warn','error']).default('info')
  - NODE_ENV: z.enum(['development','production','test']).default('development')
- Export parsed config object — throws with clear Zod error if any required var is missing
- Replaces loadEnv.ts — no process.exit(), throw instead

src/openapi.ts (OpenAPI registry — part of this task)
- Call extendZodWithOpenApi(z) once (this module is the single place this call lives)
- Create and export a shared OpenAPIRegistry instance
- Export generateOpenAPIDocument(): OpenAPIObject that calls OpenApiGeneratorV31 with:
  - openapi: '3.1.0'
  - info: { title: 'vex-chat spire', version: '0.1.0' }
  - servers: [{ url: 'http://localhost:{port}', variables: { port: { default: '16777' } } }]
- Export bearerAuth security scheme component registered on the registry

scripts/generate-openapi.ts (generation script — part of this task)
- Imports all route files (to trigger registry.registerPath() calls)
- Calls generateOpenAPIDocument()
- Writes openapi.json to project root with JSON.stringify(doc, null, 2)
- Add 'generate:openapi' and 'lint:openapi' scripts to package.json:
  - 'generate:openapi': 'node --import tsx/esm scripts/generate-openapi.ts'
  - 'lint:openapi': 'spectral lint openapi.json --ruleset .spectral.yaml'
title: Implement utility functions (logger, UUID, config/env via Zod)

close_reason: Tests written and failing correctly. 9/11 fail because migration stubs are empty (expected). 2 pass (idempotency + empty migrateDown). Ready for vex-chat-b5e.
description:
Write vitest tests for the Kysely schema migrations before any migrations are implemented.

Tests to cover:
- Running migrations up on a fresh in-memory SQLite DB creates all 11 tables with correct columns
- Running migrations down cleanly removes all tables
- Column types and constraints are correct (uniqueness on username, signKey; foreign key owner → users)
- Idempotency: running migrations twice does not error
- Verify indexes exist on: userID in preKeys/oneTimeKeys, recipient in mail, serverID in channels, userID+resourceID in permissions, owner in files/emojis, serverID in invites

Setup: use better-sqlite3 in-memory mode (':memory:') so tests are fast and isolated. Each test gets a fresh DB instance.
title: [TEST] DB schema migration tests

description:
Write vitest integration tests for all HTTP routes before implementing them.

Use supertest + a real express app wired to an in-memory SQLite Kysely instance.

Test groups:
- Auth routes: POST /register creates user + returns JWT cookie; POST /auth returns JWT on valid creds, 401 on invalid; POST /whoami returns current user; POST /goodbye clears cookie
- Token routes: GET /token/:type requires auth, returns scoped token; invalid types rejected
- User routes: GET /user/:id returns censored user (no passwordHash); GET /user/:id/devices returns device list
- Device routes: POST /user/:id/devices registers new device; GET /device/:id/otk/count returns count; POST /device/:id/otk saves OTKs
- Server routes: POST /server/:name creates server (requires auth); GET /server/:id returns server; DELETE /server/:id requires DELETE power level
- Channel routes: POST /server/:id/channels creates channel; GET /server/:id/channels lists channels
- Permission routes: GET /server/:serverID/permissions lists perms; DELETE /permission/:id requires DELETE power
- Invite routes: POST /server/:serverID/invites requires INVITE power; GET lists invites
- File/avatar/emoji routes: basic upload + serve roundtrip
- Auth guards: all protected routes return 401 without valid JWT
title: [TEST] HTTP API integration tests

description:
Write vitest tests for the permission system before implementation.

Test cases (in-memory SQLite via Kysely):
- createPermission(userID, resourceType, resourceID, powerLevel) inserts permission
- getPermissions(userID) returns all permissions for a user
- getPermissionsByResource(resourceID) returns all permissions for a resource
- deletePermission(permissionID) removes the permission
- getGroupMembers(channelID) returns users who have permission on the channel's server
- Power level checks:
  - hasPermission(db, userID, resourceID, minPowerLevel) returns true/false
  - User with powerLevel=50 satisfies minPowerLevel=25
  - User with powerLevel=25 does NOT satisfy minPowerLevel=50
  - User with no permission entry returns false for any check
title: [TEST] Permission/RBAC tests

description:
Write vitest tests for pre-key and one-time-key management before implementation.

Test cases (in-memory SQLite via Kysely):
- savePreKey(userID, deviceID, publicKey, signature, index) stores key
- getPreKeys(userID) returns all pre-keys for a user
- saveOTK(userID, deviceID, publicKey, signature, index) stores one-time key
- getOTK(deviceID) returns one OTK and deletes it (one-time use)
- getOTKCount(deviceID) returns count of remaining OTKs
- getKeyBundle(deviceID) returns { device, preKey, otk } — OTK is consumed (deleted)
- getKeyBundle returns otk: null if no OTKs remain (graceful degradation)
- publicKey must be 64 hex chars — invalid keys are rejected
title: [TEST] Cryptographic key management tests

description:
Implement user authentication logic in src/auth/.

Functions to implement:
- hashPassword(password: string): Promise<{ hash: string; salt: string }> — PBKDF2 SHA-512, 1000 iterations, random salt
- verifyPassword(password: string, hash: string, salt: string): Promise<boolean>
- registerUser(db: Kysely<Database>, username: string, password: string): Promise<User> — validates username against /^(\w{3,19})$/, hashes password, inserts row
- loginUser(db: Kysely<Database>, username: string, password: string): Promise<string | null> — verifies password, returns signed JWT on success
- generateToken(userID: string, tokenType: TokenType): string — signed JWT with 10-minute expiry, scoped to token type
- validateToken(token: string, tokenType: TokenType): { valid: boolean; userID?: string }

JWT: 7-day expiry for session tokens, stored in httpOnly cookie named 'token'. Uses SPK from config as secret.

Zod schemas (src/auth/schemas.ts):
- RegisterBodySchema: { username: z.string(), password: z.string().min(8) }
- LoginBodySchema: same
- JWTPayloadSchema: { userID: z.string().uuid(), username: z.string(), iat: z.number(), exp: z.number() }
- TokenPayloadSchema: { userID: z.string().uuid(), type: TokenTypeSchema, iat: z.number(), exp: z.number() }

Uses: pbkdf2 (promisified), jsonwebtoken, SPK from config
title: Implement user authentication

description:
Reimplement the vex-chat/spire chat server from scratch in apps/spire. The goal is NOT a line-for-line port — it's a clean reimplementation of the core protocol logic using modern tooling.

Reference: github.com/vex-chat/spire (for understanding the protocol/behavior)
Target: apps/spire in this pnpm monorepo (TypeScript, ESM, Node 20+)

Tech choices:
- Kysely: type-safe SQL query builder (replaces Knex). Kysely Database interface drives all table types.
- Zod: schema and type validation for env vars, request bodies, and API I/O
- SQLite (better-sqlite3) or PostgreSQL (pg) depending on DB_TYPE env var — no MySQL
- ws: WebSocket server (no express-ws)
- express@5: HTTP server
- pino: logging
- tweetnacl: NaCl signing for WebSocket challenge-response auth
- pbkdf2: password hashing (SHA-512, 1000 iterations, per-user salt)
- jsonwebtoken: JWT auth (7-day expiry, httpOnly cookies)
- @msgpack/msgpack: message serialization (replaces msgpack-lite)
- vitest: testing (in-memory SQLite for test isolation)

Core domains to implement (each with tests-first):
1. DB schema (Kysely migrations, 11 tables)
2. Utility functions (logger, UUID, env/config via Zod)
3. User authentication (register, login, JWT, scoped tokens)
4. Device management (register, revoke, list)
5. Cryptographic key management (pre-keys, OTKs, key bundles)
6. Mail system (encrypted mail between devices)
7. Server & channel management
8. Permission/RBAC
9. File, avatar & emoji storage
10. Invite system
11. WebSocket connection manager
12. HTTP API routes
13. Application entry point
title: [EPIC] Reimplement vex-chat/spire core logic in apps/spire
